topic: Automatic Memory Extraction from LLM Conversations

sources.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
keyInsights: Three-tier memory: core (RAM-like), archival (disk-like), recall (conversation history)
Memory management functions: core_memory_append, core_memory_replace, archival_memory_insert, archival_memory_search, conversation_search
OS-inspired virtual context management paging information between tiers
LLM autonomously decides when to read/write memory via tool calls
Enables context beyond native window size

sources.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
keyInsights: Memory stream: complete record of all experiences in natural language
Retrieval scoring combines recency + importance + relevance (three dimensions)
Reflection mechanism synthesizes observations into higher-level insights
Planning uses retrieved memories and reflections to generate future actions
Ablations show all three components (observation, planning, reflection) are critical
Emergent social behaviors from simple memory architecture (e.g., party coordination)

sources.title: Zep: A Temporal Knowledge Graph Architecture for Agent Memory
keyInsights: Graphiti engine: temporally-aware knowledge graph that maintains historical relationships
Synthesizes both unstructured conversational data and structured business data
Facts: precise time-stamped information with relationships
Summaries: contextualized entity histories that update with new information
Fact invalidation: when new data supersedes old, the invalidation timestamp is stored on the edge
94.8% accuracy on Deep Memory Retrieval vs MemGPT's 93.4%
18.5% accuracy improvement on LongMemEval with 90% latency reduction
Nodes represent entities; edges represent facts/relationships

sources.title: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory (Mem0)
keyInsights: Scalable memory-centric architecture: dynamically extracts, consolidates, and retrieves salient information
Two-stage LLM pipeline: fact extraction then ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP decision
Vector similarity search to find existing memories before deciding action
Graph-based variant captures relational structures between conversational elements
26% relative improvement on LLM-as-a-Judge vs OpenAI
91% lower p95 latency vs full-context approaches
90%+ token cost reduction
Evaluated on single-hop, temporal, multi-hop, and open-domain questions

sources.title: Mem0 GitHub Repository - main.py implementation
keyInsights: Uses gpt-4.1-nano-2025-04-14 for fact extraction by default, text-embedding-3-small for vectors
get_fact_retrieval_messages() generates extraction prompts based on agent vs user context
LLM returns JSON { "facts": [...] } array; robust parsing handles malformed JSON
normalize_facts() post-processes extracted facts
Vector similarity retrieves existing memories; temporary UUIDs prevent hallucinations in update prompt
get_update_memory_messages() submits old+new facts to LLM for ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NONE decisions
Stores in Qdrant vector store + SQLite history database

sources.title: Mem0 Python Quickstart Documentation
keyInsights: m.add(messages, user_id=user_id) extracts facts from conversation
m.search(query, filters={...}) semantic retrieval of relevant memories
Memory scoped to user_id, agent_id, or session_id
Three memory types: User (persistent), Agent (context-specific), Session (conversation-specific)
Multimodal support and custom categories available

sources.title: Zep Documentation - Context Engineering Platform
keyInsights: Temporal knowledge graph engine for dynamic fact and entity extraction
User Graphs: per-user knowledge graphs with facts and summaries
Group Graphs: shared graphs for non-user-specific information
Threads: conversation containers that trigger automatic extraction
thread.get_user_context() returns optimized 'Context Block' with summaries + relevant facts
graph.search() for low-level graph queries
Custom entity and edge types via Pydantic-like classes
User Summary Instructions: up to 5 directives per user to guide summary generation
200ms retrieval latency

sources.title: LangGraph Memory Concepts Documentation
keyInsights: Short-term (thread-scoped): conversation history within sessions via checkpointers
Long-term (cross-thread): user/application data across conversations in custom namespaces
Three memory types: Semantic (facts/profiles), Episodic (past experiences as few-shot examples), Procedural (rules/instructions)
Profile approach: single continuously updated document (risk of data loss)
Collection approach: multiple scoped documents appended over time (better recall, needs careful management)
Hot path writing: real-time updates (higher latency, immediate availability)
Background writing: async updates (lower latency, requires scheduling logic)

sources.title: LangGraph Add Memory Tutorial
keyInsights: Store API: runtime.store.asearch(namespace, query=...) for semantic search
runtime.store.aput(namespace, id, data) for persisting memories
Namespace tuple pattern: (user_id, 'memories') for isolation
Semantic search via embeddings: InMemoryStore(index={'embed': embeddings, 'dims': 1536})
context_schema pattern for injecting user context at runtime
Production backends: PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB (require .setup())
Short-term via checkpointer; long-term via store - both on same compiled graph

sources.title: LangChain Blog: Memory for Agents
keyInsights: Procedural memory: LLM weights + agent code (rarely updated directly)
Semantic memory: factual knowledge for personalization (user profiles, preferences)
Episodic memory: sequences of past actions as few-shot examples
In-the-hot-path: agent explicitly decides to remember before responding (adds latency)
In-the-background: async memory updates (no latency hit, requires scheduling logic)
User feedback mechanism: marking interactions positively preserves them for recall
Memory is application-specific: different agents need different extraction criteria

sources.title: LangChain Conversational Memory Types - Pinecone Guide
keyInsights: ConversationBufferMemory: raw history, maximum context but unsustainable at scale
ConversationSummaryMemory: LLM-powered progressive compression, good for long sessions
ConversationBufferWindowMemory: sliding window of k recent turns, constant token budget
ConversationSummaryBufferMemory: hybrid - summarize old + buffer recent (best of both)
All integrate via {history} template parameter in ConversationChain
Entity/relationship extraction uses separate NER-like prompts to build entity store

sources.title: MemoryBank: Enhancing Large Language Models with Long-Term Memory
keyInsights: Ebbinghaus Forgetting Curve: memory strength decays over time based on elapsed time and significance
Memory reinforcement: revisiting or referencing a memory increases its strength score
Enables models to 'forget' irrelevant information and reinforce important memories
Supports both closed-source (ChatGPT) and open-source (ChatGLM) models
SiliconFriend chatbot: demonstrated emphatic responses and user personality understanding
Memory retrieval combines temporal and semantic signals

sources.title: HippoRAG: Neurobiologically Inspired Long-Term Memory for Large Language Models
keyInsights: Inspired by hippocampal indexing theory: neocortex + hippocampus analogy
LLMs + knowledge graphs + Personalized PageRank algorithm
Entity extraction to build the knowledge graph from new experiences
20% better performance than standard RAG on multi-hop QA
10-30x cheaper and 6-13x faster than iterative retrieval methods like IRCoT
Handles novel scenarios beyond existing RAG methods' capabilities

sources.title: RET-LLM: Towards a General Read-Write Memory for Large Language Models
keyInsights: Knowledge extracted and stored as triplets (Davidsonian semantics)
Four memory properties: scalable, aggregatable, updatable, interpretable
Strong performance on temporal QA and general knowledge retrieval
Basis for the more comprehensive MemLLM system
Triplet-based representation bridges structured and unstructured knowledge

sources.title: Cognitive Architectures for Language Agents (CoALA)
keyInsights: CoALA framework organizes agent architecture around cognitive science principles
Modular memory components: working memory, episodic memory, semantic memory, procedural memory
Structured action space for interacting with internal memory and external environments
Generalized decision-making process that chooses actions based on memory state
Comprehensive survey and taxonomy of existing LLM agent architectures
Actionable directions toward language-based general intelligence

sources.title: A Survey on the Memory Mechanism of Large Language Model based Agents
keyInsights: Comprehensive taxonomy of memory mechanisms in LLM-based agents
Memory essential for agent self-evolution and long-term environment interactions
Design patterns for memory construction, evaluation, and application
Four common memory storage forms: in-context, external database, in-weights, in-cache
Memory operations: read (retrieval) and write (storage, management)
Survey of 50+ memory systems with comparative analysis

sources.title: REMEMBERER: Large Language Models Are Semi-Parametric Reinforcement Learning Agents
keyInsights: Long-term experience memory updated via Reinforcement Learning with Experience Memory (RLEM)
Learns from both successes and failures without fine-tuning LLM parameters
Semi-parametric: LLM weights fixed, external memory updated via RL
4% and 2% improvement over SOTA on two RL task sets
Memory stores past task experiences as examples for future similar tasks

sources.title: Memory Sandbox: Transparent and Interactive Memory Management for Conversational Agents
keyInsights: Treats memories as data objects that can be viewed, manipulated, recorded, summarized, and shared
User-facing memory management interface for transparency and control
Addresses conversational breakdowns caused by poor memory visibility
Allows sharing memory contexts across multiple conversations
Users can directly manipulate what agents remember
Key insight: memory opacity reduces user trust in AI systems

sources.title: iAgents: Information Asymmetry in Multi-Agent LLM Networks
keyInsights: InfoNav mechanism: agents proactively exchange necessary human information for task resolution
Mixed memory structure organizes human information for accurate access
InformativeBench: first benchmark for evaluating agents under information asymmetry
Scaled to 140 individuals, 588 relationships, 70,000+ messages, 30+ communication turns
Demonstrates memory partitioning and sharing across multi-agent systems

sources.title: A Survey on Large Language Model-based Multi-Agent Systems
keyInsights: Memory mechanisms fundamental to agent cooperation and task solving
Shared memory enables agent coordination in multi-agent systems
Agent profiling, communication, and memory interconnected in multi-agent design
Memory types differ by persistence scope and access patterns across agent roles

sources.title: HuggingFace: Papers on Memory Extraction from LLM Conversations
keyInsights: CogCanvas: verbatim-grounded artifact extraction (decisions, facts, reminders), 32.4% on LoCoMo, +7.8pp over RAG
ProMem: proactive memory extraction with iterative self-questioning feedback loops
SeCom: segment-level memory construction with conversation segmentation and prompt compression
EMem: event-centric memory using elementary discourse units (EDUs) in heterogeneous graphs
MemoChat: iterative memorization-retrieval-response cycles via memo refinement
On Structural Memory: four structures (chunks, knowledge triples, atomic facts, summaries) - mixed memory resilient to noise
MemGAS: multi-granularity memory with Gaussian Mixture Model clustering and entropy-based routing
MemInsight: autonomous memory augmentation for recommendation, QA, event summarization (14% boost)

sources.title: arXiv: Recent Papers on Memory Extraction for LLM Conversations
keyInsights: Structured Distillation for Personalized Agent Memory: 11x compression, 96% retrieval quality, 4-field structured objects
TraceMem: three-stage pipeline (topic segmentation → episodic summaries → hierarchical narrative threads → user memory cards)
Hindsight is 20/20: four logical memory networks (world facts, agent experiences, entity summaries, evolving beliefs)
Bi-Mem: inductive agent extracts facts from conversations; reflective agent calibrates accuracy with global constraints
Adaptive Memory Admission Control: five-factor memory value decomposition (future utility, factual confidence, semantic novelty, temporal recency, content type prior)

sources.title: Dense X Retrieval: What Retrieval Granularity Should We Use?
keyInsights: Propositions as retrieval units: atomic expressions each encapsulating a distinct factoid in self-contained natural language
Fine-grained retrieval units (propositions) significantly outperform passage-level indexing
Proposition extraction from source text improves downstream QA within context constraints
Granularity choice impacts both retrieval quality and downstream task performance
Atomic factoid units enable more precise memory storage and retrieval

sources.title: Mem0 Homepage
keyInsights: Positions as 'Universal, Self-improving memory layer for LLM applications'
Three products: Mem0 Platform (managed), Mem0 Open Source (self-hosted), OpenMemory (team workspace)
MCP protocol integration for universal AI client compatibility
Integrates with LangChain, CrewAI, Vercel AI SDK

sources.title: Zep Concepts Documentation
keyInsights: Graph nodes represent entities; edges represent facts and relationships
Dynamic graph updates in response to new data continuously
Fact invalidation: edge stores the timestamp when fact became invalid
Processes JSON, text, and message formats from diverse sources
Custom entity/edge types via Pydantic-like class definitions
Two retrieval paths: low-level graph.search() and high-level thread.get_user_context()

sources.title: Letta (formerly MemGPT) GitHub Repository
keyInsights: Memory blocks as fundamental storage: 'human' block (user info) + 'persona' block (agent identity)
Tool calling interface for memory operations: web_search, fetch_webpage, memory management
Model-agnostic: supports various LLMs including GLM, GPT, Opus
Skills and subagents for composable functionality
Python and TypeScript SDKs
Stateful agent persistence enables continuous learning across sessions

sources.title: LangGraph Memory Documentation
keyInsights: JSON document storage with hierarchical namespace:key organization
InMemoryStore with embedding index for semantic search
Runtime object injection pattern for accessing store in nodes
PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB backends for production scale
Memory categories map to cognitive science: semantic, episodic, procedural

sources.title: MemGPT Research Site
keyInsights: UC Berkeley team released code and experimental data
Virtual context management: paging between fast and slow memory inspired by OS
LLM teaches itself to manage its own memory via interrupt-driven control flow
Validated on document analysis (beyond context window) and multi-session chat
Agents can 'remember, reflect, and evolve dynamically through long-term interactions'

sources.title: MemGPT Paper (PDF): Full Technical Details
keyInsights: Core memory: limited, immediately active (like CPU registers/cache)
Archival memory: unlimited persistent external storage (like disk)
Recall memory: searchable log of past conversation messages
Agent autonomously decides to page info in/out using tool calls
Interrupts manage control flow when memory operations are needed mid-response
Two primary use cases: long document QA and multi-session personalized chat

sources.title: Generative Agents Paper (Park et al. 2023) - Full Details
keyInsights: 25 agents in Smallville sandbox demonstrate emergent social behavior
Memory stream stores all observations as natural language with timestamps
Retrieval: score = recency_score + importance_score + relevance_score (weighted sum, all normalized 0-1)
Importance scoring: LLM rates each observation 1-10 for 'poignancy' (memorability)
Reflection: periodically asks 'What are 3 most salient questions based on recent memories?' then answers them
Planning: converts reflections into daily plans decomposed into hourly then 5-min actions
All three layers (observe, plan, reflect) needed for believable behavior in ablation

sources.title: Microsoft Research Blog: Generative Agents
keyInsights: Memory stream architecture validated at Microsoft Research
Emergent social behavior demonstrates effectiveness of memory + reflection
Natural language memory storage enables reasoning without structured schemas

sources.title: Mem0 Documentation Overview
keyInsights: Graph memory, webhooks, multimodal support, custom categories as advanced features
Rerankers for improving retrieval quality
Open source self-hosted and managed cloud options
REST API + Python/TypeScript SDKs

sources.title: LangGraph Add Memory How-To
keyInsights: Compile graph with both checkpointer (short-term) and store (long-term)
runtime.store.asearch() uses semantic similarity when embeddings configured
runtime.store.aput(namespace, str(uuid.uuid4()), {"data": "..."}) to persist
Context schema injected at runtime for per-user memory isolation
setup() required for DB-backed stores (PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB)

sources.title: iAgents: NeurIPS 2024 - Information Asymmetry in Multi-Agent Systems
keyInsights: NeurIPS 2024 accepted paper on agent memory in social networks
Mixed memory structure: combines user information from multiple sources
InfoNav: proactive information exchange guided by task requirements
Scales to realistic social network sizes

sources.title: MemoryBank Paper - Ebbinghaus Forgetting Curve
keyInsights: Memory strength = f(time_elapsed, significance) following forgetting curve
Strength decays exponentially over time without reinforcement
Significance score set at memory creation time based on content importance
Revisiting or using a memory reinforces it (increases strength)
Below-threshold memories eventually 'forgotten' (not retrieved)
Tested with SiliconFriend: empathic responses, personality understanding

sources.title: RET-LLM: General Read-Write Memory via Knowledge Triplets
keyInsights: Triplet extraction: (subject, predicate, object) + event information
Davidsonian event semantics for richer triplet structure
Scalable: can grow indefinitely without retraining
Aggregatable: merges information across triplets for coherent answers
Updatable: individual triplets can be modified without affecting others
Interpretable: humans can read and verify stored triplets

sources.title: CoALA: Cognitive Architectures for Language Agents
keyInsights: Working memory: in-context information for current task (temporary)
Episodic memory: logs of past experiences with retrieval for few-shot learning
Semantic memory: world knowledge and facts (structured or unstructured)
Procedural memory: task execution rules encoded in weights or explicit programs
Action space: retrieval actions, reasoning actions, external environment actions
Decision procedure: planning (deliberate) vs reactive (immediate) action selection

sources.title: Survey on Memory Mechanisms of LLM-based Agents
keyInsights: Four memory storage types: in-context (working), external database, in-weights (parametric), in-cache (KV cache)
Read operations: retrieval by semantic similarity, keyword, structured query, recency, random
Write operations: storage, fusion (dedup/contradiction), forgetting (decay/deletion), updating
Memory types compared: in-context fastest but limited; external most scalable; in-weights most permanent
Evaluation on long-context tasks, multi-session QA, task completion across diverse agent apps

sources.title: LangChain Blog: Memory For Agents (Full Details)
keyInsights: Memory is application-specific: one size does not fit all
LangChain's memory store abstractions support all three writing patterns
Dynamic few-shot selection tools for episodic memory implementation
Templates for both hot-path and background extraction
User feedback (thumbs up/down) as memory extraction trigger

sources.title: HippoRAG: Neurobiologically Inspired Memory Architecture
keyInsights: OpenIE-based named entity extraction to build knowledge graph nodes
Pairing KG with vector embeddings for hybrid retrieval
Personalized PageRank seeds from query entities, propagates to related nodes
Hippocampal indexing: hippocampus stores indexes to neocortical memories
Phrase-level nodes in KG enable fine-grained entity matching
Combines well with other retrieval methods in an ensemble

sources.title: Dense X Retrieval: Proposition-Level Memory Units
keyInsights: Propositions defined as atomic, self-contained factoid expressions
LLM extracts propositions from text chunks during indexing phase
Proposition-level outperforms sentence-level and passage-level in retrieval
Fine-grained units allow more precise memory content without context loss
Applicable to conversation memory: each statement can be stored as proposition

sources.title: Memory Sandbox (2023) - User-Controlled Memory Management
keyInsights: Memory as interactive data objects (not opaque internal state)
Three operations: summarize (compress), manipulate (edit), share (export/import)
Highlights gap between agent memory capability and user mental models
Shared memory contexts across conversations without manual re-entry
User study showed improved trust and task success with transparent memory

sources.title: Long-Context Conversational Memory Retrieval (Time/Event-Based QA)
keyInsights: Time/event-based memory queries require specialized retrieval (not just semantic similarity)
Ambiguous queries need surrounding conversational context to interpret correctly
Novel dataset of ambiguous and time-based questions from conversational data
Chain-of-table search + vector retrieval + disambiguation prompting
Standard RAG fails on temporal and event-ordered memory queries
Temporal reasoning crucial for realistic conversational memory

sources.title: REMEMBERER: Semi-Parametric RL Agents with Experience Memory
keyInsights: RL-updated external memory without touching LLM parameters
Memory stores task-specific experiences for future retrieval
Learns to prefer successful strategies from past episodes
Semi-parametric: best of both worlds (LLM reasoning + updatable experience)
Demonstrates memory as a learnable, improving component
